user_message: Just got this from Michael Petrov at Humongous Insurance -- I need help figuring out competitive positioning. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 11:18 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 11:20 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 14:39 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-L trap (Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-L failure mode (Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-L -- Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
